title: Escrow title is required but discarded on submit

evidence.quote: const [form, setForm] = useState({ title: "", beneficiary: "", amount: "" });
args: [form.beneficiary as `0x${string}`, resolvedArbiter, descriptions, amounts, 0, useAIArbiter, referrer],
<Field label={t("titleLabel")} value={form.title} onChange={v => setForm(f => ({ ...f, title: v }))} placeholder={t("titlePlaceholder")} />

reasoning: The page collects a required title into form.title, but neither createSimpleEscrow nor createMilestoneEscrow receives it, and this route does not otherwise persist it before navigating to the created escrow. A user-entered required field is therefore lost as soon as the transaction is submitted.
reproduction: Fill in a title, deploy either simple or milestone escrow, and inspect the submitted contract args or destination route state. The title is not included anywhere in the submit path.
recommendation: Either persist the title through the intended metadata/storage path before navigation, include it in the relevant contract/API call if supported, or remove the field/required constraint if titles are not supported.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests exercise form submission payloads or verify that all required user inputs are persisted.
suggestedRegressionTest: Submit the create form with a unique title and assert the expected persistence mechanism receives that title for both simple and milestone escrow flows.
minimumFixScope: Define the intended title persistence contract and update frontend/src/app/[locale]/create/page.tsx submit handling accordingly.
